title: Beyond Good and Evil
subtitle: Prelude to a Philosophy of the Future
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
originalTitle: Jenseits von Gut und Böse. Vorspiel einer Philosophie der Zukunft
themes: Critique of dogmatic philosophy and moral prejudice
The will to power as the fundamental drive of all life
Perspectivism — all knowledge is interpretation from a standpoint
Beyond conventional good and evil to a life-affirming ethics
The death of God and the need for new values
The pathos of distance and order of rank among souls
Self-overcoming as the highest human calling
awards: Widely regarded as one of the most influential philosophical works of the 19th century
Included in Modern Library's 100 Best Nonfiction Books
Influenced existentialism, postmodernism, psychoanalysis, and critical theory
excerpt: Beyond Good and Evil is Nietzsche's most mature philosophical manifesto — a relentless critique of dogmatic philosophy, religious morality, and the prejudice for truth, culminating in his vision of new philosophers who create values beyond the ancient categories of good and evil.
